general_vehicle_dynamics_validated.reason: CF-M 001 covers only ConstantPlanner straight-line constant-speed state evolution; it does not exercise general acceleration, braking, steering, tire-road dynamics, or externally qualified vehicle bounds.

general_hugsim_ad_test_domain_fitness.reason: The audit uses simulator-produced states in one scene with one scripted actor and does not test real-world state truth, perception, planning, control, or closed-loop AD outcomes.

constant_speed_vector_integration_holds.component: HUGSIM saved actor state plus independently recomputed kinematics
constant_speed_vector_integration_holds.expected: Every adjacent state transition follows the declared constant speed and actor heading within preregistered numerical tolerances.
constant_speed_vector_integration_holds.observed: All 108 transitions pass; maximum vector integration residual is 1.64e-8 m versus the 1e-5 m tolerance, with zero speed, heading, and acceleration residual violations.
constant_speed_vector_integration_holds.implication: The saved ConstantPlanner state sequence is internally consistent with its declared constant-speed mechanism, without establishing independent real-world state truth.

relative_motion_orders_hold.component: paired HUGSIM ego/actor state plus independent geometry
relative_motion_orders_hold.expected: Faster same-direction actors travel farther and retain larger ego-relative forward gaps and footprint clearances under identical ego trajectories.
relative_motion_orders_hold.observed: All 110 accumulated relation-check timestamps across the three preregistered order checks follow the expected direction, with zero reversals or ties; this count is not an independent statistical sample size.
relative_motion_orders_hold.implication: The speed intervention produces the expected relative-motion direction within the declared design, not a universal speed-to-risk relation.

state_source_dependence.expected: Internal mechanism evidence remains separated from independent real-world state evidence.
state_source_dependence.observed: Metrics are independently recomputed, but all actor and ego states originate from HUGSIM and the controller is scripted.
state_source_dependence.implication: The result qualifies an internal mechanism check only and cannot establish real vehicle dynamics or AD test-domain fitness.

claim_boundary: ConstantPlanner constant-speed internal state and controlled relative-motion ordering only; no general dynamics, behavior realism, receiver, AD safety, real-sim equivalence, or general simulator-fitness claim.
